top_signals.title: Stocks Extend Rally in AI-Led Rebound; Trump Says Peace Talks on Track | Bloomberg Brief 6/9/2026
top_signals.summary: US stocks enter a second day of recovery led by AI. Oil falls as President Trump claims that a US-Iran peace deal is close to fruition. China is preparing to spend around $295 billion over the next five years on building data centers across the country. Henrietta Pacquement of Allspring Global Investment looks at the Fed's rate path. Wei Li of BlackRock discusses how AI is reshaping markets. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: How Poland’s Orlen kept supplies flowing during the Middle East war
top_signals.summary: Group’s head oil trader said the company learned lessons from the 2022 shock

top_signals.title: Iran War Derails Saudi Contractor’s $799 Million Riyadh Listing
top_signals.summary: Saudi Arabia’s Mutlaq Al-Ghowairi Contracting Co. has postponed plans for an initial public offering, due in part to concerns over the impact of the Iran war, in the latest blow to regional capital markets activity.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump is becoming Jimmy Carter
top_signals.summary: Like the 39th US president, the current occupant of the White House has handed control of the narrative to Iran
